review_text: Spotify is a great app for music. I use it basically everyday for hours at a time with no issue. My one problem with it is that the amount of ads on the free version is ridiculous. It’s basically every four or five songs you get three ads. And I would get premium but that’s also ridiculously priced for individual plans. Spotify used to be great, but now I’m finding the app incredibly confusing. There are so many tabs, and some of them seem to appear more than once. I also have to scroll endlessly through album icons that I never selected. I’m talking pages and pages of them. The user experience is a mess. And what the hell is the new icon anyway? When I search for something, I get endless results that have nothing to do with what I’m looking for. Classical music is especially a nightmare. If a piece has more than one movement, I have to click on every single track just to hear it or see the full title before I can choose the one I want. The only reason I’m still using Spotify is because I’ve built playlists over the last 10 years. Ironically, those playlists are so hard to find that I can spend the first three minutes in the app just trying to locate them. I’d much rather see my own playlist icons than random miscellaneous junk I don’t care about. It would be so nice if the app were simpler, cleaner, and actually useful. Spotify, we can do better.
